questions.question: How long must a user be inactive before their free user account is considered dormant?

options.text: 1 year
options.explanation: An account is considered dormant after five years, not one year.

options.text: 5 years
options.explanation: According to the policy, a free user account is dormant if inactive for five years.

options.text: 10 years
options.explanation: The policy specifies five years of inactivity.

options.text: 90 days
options.explanation: The 90-day period refers to the notice before deletion, not the duration of inactivity.

questions.question: What specific condition must be met for a workspace to be deemed dormant?

options.text: If the workspace has no content at all
options.explanation: Content presence is not the primary factor; it is based on user ownership status.

options.text: If the workspace has no active members
options.explanation: The rule specifically looks at the status of the owners, not general members.

options.text: If all the owners of the workspace have a dormant user account
options.explanation: The policy states workspaces are deemed dormant if all owners have dormant accounts.

options.text: If the workspace was created more than 10 years ago
options.explanation: Age of creation does not determine dormancy; activity does.

questions.question: Which of the following actions resets the clock on a dormant account?

options.text: Logging in once a year
options.explanation: Logging in alone is not enough; you must also edit or create content.

options.text: Creating a new page
options.explanation: While creating content is required, you must also log in to reset the inactivity timer.

options.text: Logging in and editing or creating any content
options.explanation: Inactivity is defined as failing to log in AND failing to edit/create content.

options.text: Inviting a new collaborator to the workspace
options.explanation: Inviting collaborators does not count as activity by the account owner.

questions.question: Who does NOT receive an email notification before their account is terminated?

options.text: Free user accounts
options.explanation: Free accounts are the primary targets for the dormant policy and receive notifications.

options.text: Guest accounts
options.explanation: The policy explicitly states the notification is not sent if the account does not belong to a guest.

options.text: Workspace owners
options.explanation: Workspace owners are typically the ones with the active accounts requiring notification.

options.text: Premium user accounts
options.explanation: Premium accounts are subject to the same dormant policies as free accounts.

questions.question: How much notice is given before a dormant account and its corresponding workspaces are terminated?

options.text: 7 days
options.explanation: The notice period is longer than one week.

options.text: 30 days
options.explanation: The notice period is longer than one month.

options.text: 90 days
options.explanation: The policy specifies a ninety (90) day period lapses without activity before termination.

options.text: 180 days
options.explanation: The standard notice period is 90 days, not 180.

questions.question: A workspace has two owners. Owner A has been inactive for 4 years, but logs in and edits content today. Owner B has been inactive for 6 years. What happens to the workspace?

options.text: The workspace will be deleted immediately because Owner B is dormant.
options.explanation: The deletion process is based on the 90-day notice period, not immediate action based on owner status.

options.text: The workspace is not considered dormant because Owner A is active.
options.explanation: Workspaces are dormant only if **all** owners have dormant accounts. Since Owner A is active, the workspace is not dormant.

options.text: The workspace will be deleted because it has been inactive for a total of 10 years.
options.explanation: Inactivity is measured per account, not summed across all owners.

options.text: The workspace is deleted if Owner B’s account is deleted within the next 90 days.
options.explanation: The workspace status is determined by the current state of the owners, not a future hypothetical deletion.

questions.question: Which of the following is true regarding the '90-day period' mentioned in the deletion process?

options.text: It is the amount of time an account must be inactive before deletion.
options.explanation: The 90-day period is the window for activity after notification, not the duration of inactivity.

options.text: It starts immediately when the account is identified as dormant.
options.explanation: The period starts after the notification is sent (or if no notification is sent to a guest).

options.text: If any activity occurs within this window, the account remains active indefinitely.
options.explanation: The text states that if activity occurs prior to the date the 90-day period lapses, the account and workspaces will not be terminated.

options.text: It allows the user to delete the account themselves.
options.explanation: The period is for Notion to monitor activity, not for the user to perform actions.
